content:
Warrior Trading Pricing Overview

Warrior Trading is a day trading education platform founded in 2012 by Ross Cameron that offers three pricing tiers: a free Starter plan, a mid-tier Warrior Pro$197/month, and a premium Inner Circle tier at $297/month. The platform focuses on small-cap momentum trading, live chat rooms, real-time alerts, and a simulated trading environment for practicing without risking capital. Whether you're exploring day trading fundamentals or seeking advanced education and community access, Warrior Trading positions itself as a comprehensive solution—but at a cost that separates serious students from casual browsers.

Founded in Great Barrington, Massachusetts, Warrior Trading has built its reputation on Ross Cameron's verified trading track record and an active community of traders sharing strategies during live market hours. However, the platform's aggressive pricing strategy and promotion of high-risk trading strategies mean it's not for everyone. Let's break down exactly what you pay for at each level and whether the investment makes sense for your trading goals.

All Warrior Trading Plans Compared

Warrior Trading offers three distinct membership levels, each designed for different experience levels and commitment levels. Here's a detailed breakdown:

Feature Starter (Free) Warrior Pro Inner Circle
Monthly Cost $0 $197/month $297/month
Annual Cost $0 ~$2,364/year ~$3,564/year
Live Chat Room Access Limited ✓ Full Access ✓ Full Access
Structured Courses Basic ✓ All Courses ✓ All Courses
Paper Trading Simulator ✓ Access ✓ Access ✓ Access
Real-Time Trade Alerts ✗ ✓ Yes ✓ Yes
Mobile App Limited ✓ Full Access ✓ Full Access
1-on-1 Mentoring ✗ ✗ ✓ Included
Priority Support Community Only Standard ✓ Priority
Webinars & Workshops Recording Access ✓ Live + Recording ✓ Live + Recording
Risk Management Tools Basic ✓ Advanced ✓ Advanced

Starter Plan ($0/month) — The free tier provides foundational access: basic course content, paper trading simulator for practice, and limited chat room access. However, you won't receive real-time trade alerts or full course materials. This tier is ideal for testing whether day trading education appeals to you before committing funds.

Warrior Pro ($197/month or ~$2,364/year) — The mid-tier membership unlocks the full course library, real-time trade alerts during market hours, full live chat room access, and the mobile app. This is where most serious students land. Annual billing may offer slight savings, though Warrior Trading's website doesn't explicitly advertise an annual discount.

Inner Circle ($297/month or ~$3,564/year) — The premium tier adds one-on-one mentoring sessions with experienced traders, priority customer support, and early access to new features and workshops. This tier targets traders ready to invest heavily in personalized guidance alongside community learning.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Warrior Trading's free Starter plan is genuinely free—no credit card required. This is a meaningful commitment to accessibility, but with clear limitations. You get:

  • Access to foundational educational content and beginner courses
  • Paper trading simulator to practice without real money
  • Limited access to the live chat room (read-only or delayed access)
  • Community forums to ask questions

What's not included:

  • Real-time trade alerts and live scanner notifications
  • Advanced courses and structured curriculum
  • Full chat room participation during market hours
  • Mobile app with push notifications
  • Direct access to mentors or priority support

The free tier works well as a trial if you want to evaluate whether Warrior Trading's teaching style and community fit your learning needs. However, the lack of real-time alerts and full course access means you're experiencing a significantly limited version of the platform. Most traders interested in day trading seriously will need to upgrade to Warrior Pro within 2–3 weeks of starting.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Beyond the monthly subscription, traders using Warrior Trading will encounter additional costs not always highlighted on the pricing page:

  • Broker Fees & Commissions: Warrior Trading doesn't charge trading fees, but your broker will. If you use Interactive Brokers, TD Ameritrade, or TradeStation, expect per-share commissions ($0.005–$0.01 per share) on your actual trades. Paper trading is free, but real money trading costs compound.
  • Data Feeds & Real-Time Quotes: Some traders prefer premium market data (Level 2 quotes, advanced charting). These may cost $50–$150/month depending on your broker and data provider choice.
  • Advanced Courses & Specialized Content: While the Warrior Pro membership includes course access, Warrior Trading occasionally upsells specialized workshops, trading strategy deep-dives, or advanced mentoring packages at premium rates ($500–$5,000+).
  • Third-Party Tools: Many traders add scanners, charting software, or research tools like Finviz Elite ($40/mo) or Thinkorswim Premium for enhanced functionality.
  • Capital Requirements: Day trading requires a minimum $25,000 account balance under SEC Regulation T (PDT rule). This is not a fee, but a regulatory minimum that impacts your ability to trade.

The platform's marketing emphasizes low-cost education, but the reality is that trading with Warrior Trading's strategies typically involves thousands of dollars in annual trading costs once you move from paper to live trading.

Warrior Trading Pricing vs Competitors

How does Warrior Trading stack up against other day trading education platforms? Here's an honest comparison:

Platform Starter Price Premium Tier Key Differentiator
Warrior Trading Free $297/month Ross Cameron's track record; live chat during market hours
Thinkorswim (TD Ameritrade) Free Free Professional-grade platform; minimal education
StockStotts $99–$199/month $499/month Community-driven; fewer structured courses
Investors Underground $99/month $399/month Smaller, more exclusive community
TradeStation Education Free Free Free with broker account; limited content

Warrior Trading vs Thinkorswim: Thinkorswim is completely free and offers professional-grade trading tools, but almost no structured education. Warrior Trading costs $197–$297/month but includes live coaching, curriculum, and community. If you're motivated and self-directed, Thinkorswim wins on cost. If you want guidance, Warrior Trading's education justifies the price difference.

Warrior Trading vs StockStotts: StockStotts' entry price ($99/month) is cheaper than Warrior Pro ($197/month), but StockStotts emphasizes community-driven learning over structured courses. Warrior Trading's curriculum is more comprehensive, and Ross Cameron's public track record provides additional credibility for many students.

Warrior Trading vs Investors Underground: Investors Underground charges $99/month for entry but caps at $399/month for premium access. Warrior Trading's top tier ($297/month) sits between these, offering mentoring at a lower price point than Investors Underground's comparable tier. Both platforms have strong reputations in the momentum day trading niche.

Is Warrior Trading Worth the Price?

Whether Warrior Trading justifies its cost depends entirely on your situation:

Worth it if you:

  • Are serious about day trading and plan to trade with real capital within 3–6 months
  • Benefit from structured curriculum and prefer guided learning over self-study
  • Want live market hours coaching and real-time alerts to inform your trades
  • Value the community aspect and learn well from peers trading alongside you
  • Can commit 10+ hours/week to learning and practicing during your first year
  • Have at least $25,000 capital available for trading (SEC PDT requirement)

Not worth it if you:

  • Are uncertain whether day trading interests you; use the free tier first
  • Prefer self-directed learning and don't need community accountability
  • Already have trading experience and only need tactical alerts; consider cheaper options
  • Have less than $25,000 capital; paper trading alone won't give realistic practice
  • Are budget-conscious and can learn from free resources (YouTube, blogs, books)

Upgrade Timing: Start with the free Starter plan for 2–4 weeks. If you find yourself wanting real-time alerts, accessing courses repeatedly, or feeling limited by chat room restrictions, upgrade to Warrior Pro. The Inner Circle tier ($297/month) is worthwhile only if you're actively trading and want personalized mentoring to improve your edge. Many traders never need Inner Circle and do well with Warrior Pro alone.

How to Save on Warrior Trading

Warrior Trading's pricing is relatively fixed, but here are realistic ways to reduce costs:

  • Annual Billing: While not explicitly advertised, ask Warrior Trading's support team about annual payment discounts. Many membership platforms offer 10–20% discounts for committing to a full year upfront. This could reduce Warrior Pro's annual cost from ~$2,364 to ~$1,900–$2,100.
  • Start Free and Commit Gradually: Use the free Starter plan for 3–4 weeks before upgrading. This ensures you're only paying for what you'll actually use and prevents subscription waste.
  • Share Access (Where Allowed): Some education platforms allow multiple users per account. Check Warrior Trading's terms—if a spouse or trading partner can share, you might split the cost.
  • Pause During Non-Trading Periods: If you plan to trade seasonally or step back during low-volatility market conditions, ask whether you can pause your subscription rather than canceling, preserving your progress and community access.
  • Evaluate Bundle Deals: Warrior Trading occasionally bundles mentoring sessions with memberships at promotional rates. Watch for seasonal offers or founder announcements.
  • Career Alternatives: Some employers or trading firms offer Warrior Trading memberships as professional development benefits. If your broker or workplace has a partnership, you might access it subsidized.

The bottom line: Warrior Trading's pricing is premium, but not outrageous compared to hiring a day trading coach ($300–$500/hour) or taking a multi-week in-person bootcamp ($3,000–$15,000). If you're serious about day trading, the education ROI is real—provided you commit to learning and practicing discipline.

For more detailed analysis, see our full Warrior Trading review or compare pricing across all trading education platforms on TradingToolsHub's trading education category.
